avo_volcano_ai.note: Very strong primary cluster, 24 verified claims. USGS July 3 2026 HANS notice, peer-reviewed VOISS-Net paper (Volcanica), and GI VolcSARvatory release all fetched and confirmed. c16 (funding) must be softened: $6.3M is a NASA Disasters Program award shared across seven proposals, not VolcSARvatory alone.

governor_ai.note: Fallback survives on 3 verbatim ADN quotes. Juneau Independent survey dropped (unfetchable). Begich 90-day/Colorado-Illinois detail is ADN paraphrase, hedge as 'ADN reports' if used.

claims.text: As of the July 3, 2026 AVO weekly update, Great Sitkin volcano was at Volcano Alert Level WATCH and Aviation Color Code ORANGE, with a slow lava eruption continuing in the summit crater.
claims.verbatim_quote: Slow eruption of lava continues within the summit crater with associated low-level seismicity.

claims.text: As of July 3, 2026, Mount Kupreanof was at Volcano Alert Level ADVISORY and Aviation Color Code YELLOW, with seismic activity and volcanic gas emissions observed.
claims.verbatim_quote: Seismic activity and volcanic gas emissions were observed at Mount Kupreanof this week.

claims.text: As of July 3, 2026, Shishaldin volcano was at Volcano Alert Level ADVISORY and Aviation Color Code YELLOW, with continuing unrest.
claims.verbatim_quote: Unrest continues with small earthquakes, tremor, infrasound signals and gas emissions detected.

claims.text: Mount Kupreanof has no real-time geophysical monitoring network and is watched primarily via satellite and regional networks.
claims.verbatim_quote: There is no real-time geophysical monitoring network at Mount Kupreanof.

claims.text: The July 3, 2026 AVO weekly update reported three Alaska volcanoes in elevated status simultaneously: Great Sitkin (Watch/Orange), Kupreanof (Advisory/Yellow), and Shishaldin (Advisory/Yellow).
claims.verbatim_quote: GREAT SITKIN ... WATCH ... ORANGE ... KUPREANOF ... ADVISORY ... YELLOW ... SHISHALDIN ... ADVISORY ... YELLOW

claims.text: AVO monitors these volcanoes with a multi-sensor network including local seismic, geodetic and infrasound sensors, satellite data, web cameras, and regional infrasound and lightning networks.
claims.verbatim_quote: Local seismic, geodetic, and infrasound sensors; satellite data; web cameras; regional infrasound and lightning networks.

claims.text: VOISS-Net is a generalized deep-learning (spectrogram-based CNN) model built to detect and classify volcano seismicity such as tremor, explosions, earthquakes and long-period events.
claims.verbatim_quote: A generalized deep learning model to detect and classify volcano seismicity

claims.text: VOISS-Net was trained on more than 270,000 spectrograms.
claims.verbatim_quote: over 270,000 spectrograms

claims.text: VOISS-Net was developed using data from seven volcanoes across three regions: five Alaskan volcanoes, Mt. Etna in Italy, and Kilauea in Hawaii.
claims.verbatim_quote: Seven volcanoes across three regions (five Alaskan volcanoes, Mt. Etna in Italy, and Kilauea in Hawai'i)

claims.text: VOISS-Net achieves 87% classification accuracy on the test set.
claims.verbatim_quote: achieves an accuracy of 87 % on the test set

claims.text: VOISS-Net was developed collaboratively by scientists at the Alaska Volcano Observatory / University of Alaska Fairbanks and the U.S. Geological Survey.
claims.verbatim_quote: David Fee, Darren Tan, John Lyons, Alicia Hotovec-Ellis, Aaron Wech, Matthew Haney
claims.note: Attribute to the paper's AVO/UAF + USGS author roster; no single-sentence affiliation quote captured.

claims.text: VolcSARvatory uses interferometric synthetic aperture radar (InSAR) to detect ground movement as small as 1 centimeter.
claims.verbatim_quote: VolcSARvatory uses interferometric synthetic aperture radar, or InSAR, to detect ground movement changes as small as 1 centimeter.

claims.text: The Alaska Volcano Observatory at UAF has used a prototype of VolcSARvatory since early 2022.
claims.verbatim_quote: The Alaska Volcano Observatory at UAF has been using a prototype of this system, named VolcSARvatory, since early 2022.

claims.text: VolcSARvatory's usefulness became apparent during an earthquake swarm at long-quiet Mount Edgecumbe volcano near Sitka on April 11, 2022.
claims.verbatim_quote: a swarm of earthquakes occurred at long-quiet Mount Edgecumbe volcano, near Sitka, Alaska, on April 11, 2022.

claims.text: VolcSARvatory is being expanded to all USGS volcano observatories; the USGS Volcano Science Center operates AVO plus four other U.S. volcano observatories (five total).
claims.verbatim_quote: operates the Alaska Volcano Observatory and four other U.S. volcano observatories
claims.note: Frame as expanding to all five USGS volcano observatories.

claims.text: The UAF VolcSARvatory expansion is one of seven NASA Disasters Program proposals that share $6.3 million over two years.
claims.verbatim_quote: The seven winning proposals, announced Dec. 20, will share $6.3 million over two years.
claims.note: SOFTEN: never say $6.3M for VolcSARvatory alone; it is shared among seven NASA projects over two years.

claims.text: Alaska contains over 130 volcanoes and volcanic fields that have been active within the last two million years.
claims.verbatim_quote: Alaska contains over 130 volcanoes and volcanic fields that have been active within the last two million years.

claims.text: About 90 Alaska volcanoes have been active in the last 10,000 years, and more than 50 have been active in historical time (since about 1760).
claims.verbatim_quote: about 90 have been active within the last 10,000 years ... and more than 50 have been active within historical time (since about 1760).

claims.text: Alaska's volcanoes sit under Pacific great-circle aircraft routes traversed by more than 50,000 people per day, and can erupt ash into that airspace.
claims.verbatim_quote: Alaska's volcanoes are distributed under Pacific great circle aircraft routes (traversed by more than 50,000 people per day), and can erupt ash clouds into commercial and recreational airspace.

claims.text: USGS defines Volcano Alert Level WATCH as heightened/escalating unrest with increased eruption potential, or an eruption underway that poses limited hazards.
claims.verbatim_quote: Volcano is exhibiting heightened or escalating unrest with increased potential of eruption, timeframe uncertain, OR eruption is underway but poses limited hazards.

claims.text: USGS defines Volcano Alert Level ADVISORY as elevated unrest above known background level, closely monitored.
claims.verbatim_quote: Volcano is exhibiting signs of elevated unrest above known background level or, after a change from a higher level, volcanic activity has decreased significantly but continues to be closely monitored for possible renewed increase.

claims.text: Great Sitkin volcano summit elevation is 5,708 feet (1,740 m).
claims.verbatim_quote: Elevation: 1740m (5708ft)

claims.text: Mount Kupreanof summit elevation is 6,217 feet (1,895 m).
claims.verbatim_quote: Elevation: 1895m (6217ft)

claims.text: Mount Edgecumbe summit elevation is 3,202 feet (976 m).
claims.verbatim_quote: Elevation: 976m (3202ft)

claims.text: Democratic gubernatorial candidate Tom Begich argued Alaska must not fall behind on AI policy.
claims.value: Alaska should not be last to act
claims.verbatim_quote: Alaska should not be last to act.

claims.text: Gubernatorial candidate Jonathan Kreiss-Tomkins warned that the AI industry has understated public risk.
claims.verbatim_quote: I worry the AI industry has downplayed the risks posed to the public.

claims.text: Kreiss-Tomkins emphasized Alaskan data sovereignty, saying Alaskans' data belongs to Alaskans alone.
claims.verbatim_quote: belongs to Alaskans alone

kill_log.why_killed: AVO per-volcano page returned truncated/404; could not confirm verbatim. Do not cite Shishaldin elevation.
Misattribution: shared among seven NASA Disasters Program proposals over two years. See c16.

kill_log.claim: Juneau Independent survey specifics (McGuire sign-off; non-responders)
kill_log.why_killed: Wix page truncated on every fetch; unverifiable.
